subject: Sociology
title: Slavery's Lasting Impact
description: A short lesson to help you apply Slavery's Lasting Impact.

hook.text: Slavery ended in 1865. But 160 years later, the counties that had the most slaves still have the worst racial gaps.

buildup.text: Slavery built America's economy. Cotton, tobacco, and sugar generated wealth that funded industrialization.

discovery.text: After emancipation, Black Americans received no land, no reparations, and no capital — just freedom with nothing.

twist.text: Sharecropping replaced slavery with debt bondage. Legal freedom didn't mean economic freedom for most Black families.

climax.text: Today's racial wealth gap traces directly to slavery's legacy: stolen labor, denied inheritance, blocked opportunity.

punchline.text: Slavery didn't just end. Its consequences compounded.

quiz.question: Why does slavery still affect racial inequality today?
options: Freed slaves received no capital, and disadvantages compounded
All effects of slavery ended with emancipation
Slavery had no economic impact on America
